mission.title: karmada: Discussion about the behavior of replica scheduling weight preference
mission.description: Discussion about the behavior of replica scheduling weight preference. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current karmada deployment
steps.description:
Verify your karmada version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
helm list -n karmada 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working karmada installation.

steps.title: Review karmada configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant karmada configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get configmap -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=karmada
```
**What would you like to be added**:
Hi fellows.

Now karmada has provided two kinds of [replica division

steps.title: Apply the fix for Discussion about the behavior of replica scheduling weightÔÇª
steps.description:
According to #730, I think we could add a new division preference instead of mixing with `Weighted` preference. `ReplicaDivisionPreferenceDispersal` divides replicas into clusters as many as possible, while respecting clusters' resource availabilities during the division.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get events -n karmada --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Discussion about the behavior of replica scheduling weightÔÇª" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: According to #730, I think we could add a new division preference instead of mixing with `Weighted` preference. `ReplicaDivisionPreferenceDispersal` divides replicas into clusters as many as possible, while respecting clusters' resource availabilities during the division.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with karmada installed or the issue environment reproducible.
